expectations.text: Identifies the add_index without algorithm: :concurrently as unsafe (blocks writes)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'add_index :orders, :status — UNSAFE. Without algorithm: :concurrently, Postgres acquires an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on orders for the entire duration of the index build, blocking all writes.'

expectations.text: Notes that disable_ddl_transaction! is required alongside algorithm: :concurrently
expectations.evidence: Migration 2 shows disable_ddl_transaction! with comment 'required: CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction'.

expectations.text: Provides corrected migration code with algorithm: :concurrently
expectations.evidence: Migration 2 shows add_index :orders, :status, algorithm: :concurrently with disable_ddl_transaction!.

expectations.text: Addresses the add_column with non-null default (either confirming safe on PG 14 with notes, or explaining manual approach)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly confirms add_column is SAFE on PG 14: 'Postgres 11+ stores a constant non-null default as catalog metadata. It does not rewrite the table — adding the column is effectively instant regardless of how many rows orders has.'

eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score. Correctly distinguishes the safe add_column (PG14 + constant default) from the unsafe add_index. Provides the corrected split migration with the required disable_ddl_transaction!.
